-module(solution_module).
-export([solve/5]).

%% solve(N, Edges, Source, Target, K) -> Threshold
%% N: number of nodes
%% Edges: [[u, v, w], ...]
%% Source: starting node
%% Target: ending node
%% K: max heavy edges allowed
solve(N, Edges, Source, Target, K) ->
    if Source == Target -> 0;
    true ->
        % 1. Collect all unique edge weights plus 0, and sort them.
        UniqueWeights = [0],
        lists:foldl(fun([U, E]) -> [E||U] end, []).(),
        lists:foldl(fun(Edge, Acc) ->
            [Edge_W || Edge = [_, _, Edge_W] <- Edges] ++ Acc
        end, []).(),
        
        AllWeights = [0 | [W || [_, _, W] <- Edges]],
        SortedWeights = lists:sort(AllWeights),
        SortedUniqueWeights = lists:delete_duplicates(SortedWeights),

        % 2. Check function: canReach(Threshold)
        can_reach(N, Edges, Source, Target, K, SortedUniqueWeights).

%% Helper to delete duplicates while maintaining order (since SortedUniqueWeights is derived from sorted list)
delete_duplicates(List) ->
    case List of
        [] -> [];
        [H|T] ->
            delete_duplicates(lists:filter(fun(X) -> X /= H end, T)), [H|R] = delete_duplicates(T) -> [H|R];
            [H]
    end.

%% Helper for the core logic: Binary Search on SortedUniqueWeights
can_reach(N, Edges, Source, Target, K, SortedUniqueWeights) ->
    BinarySearch(SortedUniqueWeights, 0, length(SortedUniqueWeights) - 1, -1, fun(Threshold) ->
        % Check if reachable with the current threshold
        check_threshold(N, Edges, Source, Target, K, Threshold)
    end).

%% Binary Search implementation
BinarySearch(List, Low, High, MinThreshold, Predicate) when Low =< High ->
    MidIndex = div(Low + High, 2),
    CurrentThreshold = List !! MidIndex,
    
    if Predicate(CurrentThreshold) =:= true ->
        % Works, store result, try smaller (left)
        NewMinThreshold = CurrentThreshold,
        NewHigh = MidIndex - 1,
        can_reach_loop(List, Low, NewHigh, NewMinThreshold, Predicate)
    ;
        % Doesn't work, need larger (right)
        NewLow = MidIndex + 1,
        can_reach_loop(List, NewLow, High, MinThreshold, Predicate)
    end.

BinarySearch(_, _, _, MinThreshold, _) ->
    MinThreshold.

% Helper to manage the recursive call structure in Erlang style
can_reach_loop(List, Low, High, MinThreshold, Predicate) when Low =< High ->
    MidIndex = div(Low + High, 2),
    CurrentThreshold = List !! MidIndex,
    
    if Predicate(CurrentThreshold) =:= true ->
        NewMinThreshold = CurrentThreshold,
        NewHigh = MidIndex - 1,
        can_reach_loop(List, Low, NewHigh, NewMinThreshold, Predicate)
    ;
        NewLow = MidIndex + 1,
        can_reach_loop(List, NewLow, High, MinThreshold, Predicate)
    end.

can_reach_loop(_, _, _, MinThreshold, _) ->
    MinThreshold.


%% State: (Node, HeavyCount)
%% Implementation of BFS/State-space search
check_threshold(N, Edges, Source, Target, K, Threshold) ->
    % reachable[node][heavy_count] = boolean (true if reachable)
    
    % Initialize reachable: N rows, K+1 columns, all false.
    Reachable = lists:replicate(N, lists:replicate(K+1, false)),
    
    % Queue: list of (Node, HeavyCount) tuples
    Queue = [ {Source, 0} ],
    
    % Set initial state
    Reachable_Init = lists:update(Reachable, Source, fun(Row) -> 
        lists:update(Row, 0, true)
    end),
    
    % BFS loop
    bfs_loop(Queue, Reachable_Init, Target, K, Edges, Threshold).

bfs_loop(Queue, Reachable, Target, K, Edges, Threshold) when Queue = [] ->
    false. % Target unreachable

bfs_loop([{U, HeavyCount}|RestQueue], Reachable, Target, K, Edges, Threshold) ->
    if U == Target ->
        true % Target reached!
    ;
        % Process neighbors
        NewQueue = [],
        NewReachable = Reachable,
        
        % Iterate over all edges to find neighbors of U
        {NewQueue, NewReachable} = lists:foldl(
            fun(Acc, Edge) ->
                case find_neighbor(U, Edge) of
                    none -> Acc;
                    {V, Weight} ->
                        IsHeavy = Weight > Threshold,
                        NextHeavyCount = HeavyCount + (if IsHeavy -> 1; 0 end),
                        
                        if NextHeavyCount =< K ->
                            % Check if state (V, NextHeavyCount) has been visited
                            IsVisited = lists:nth(V, Reachable) !! NextHeavyCount,
                            if IsVisited == false ->
                                % Visit new state
                                UpdatedReachable = lists:update(Reachable, V, fun(Row) -> 
                                    lists:update(Row, NextHeavyCount, true)
                                end),
                                UpdatedQueue = [{V, NextHeavyCount} | Acc.Queue],
                                {UpdatedQueue, UpdatedReachable}
                            ;
                                {Acc.Queue, Reachable}
                            end
                        else
                            {Acc.Queue, Reachable}
                        end
                end
            end,
            % Initial accumulator state: {Queue: [], Reachable: Reachable}
            {[], Reachable}, 
            Edges
        ),
        
        % Append new items to the rest of the queue (BFS structure)
        NewQueueList = RestQueue ++ NewQueue,
        bfs_loop(NewQueueList, NewReachable, Target, K, Edges, Threshold)
    end.

%% Finds the neighbor V and its weight W if the edge involves U.
%% Returns {V, W} or none.
find_neighbor(U, [U1, V1, W]) ->
    if U1 == U ->
        {V1, W};
    else if V1 == U ->
        {U1, W};
    else
        none
    end.
find_neighbor(_, _) ->
    none.

-module(solver_module).

solve(N, Edges, Source, Target, K) ->
    if Source == Target ->
        0;
    true ->
        % 1. Collect all unique edge weights and sort them. Include 0.
        Weights = lists:map(fun([_, _, W]) -> W end, Edges),
        UniqueWeights = lists:sort(lists:unique([0 | Weights])),

        % 2. Perform binary search on UniqueWeights
        BinarySearch(UniqueWeights, 0, length(UniqueWeights) - 1, -1, N, Edges, Source, Target, K).

BinarySearch(UniqueWeights, Low, High, MinThreshold, N, Edges, Source, Target, K) when Low > High ->
    MinThreshold;
BinarySearch(UniqueWeights, Low, High, MinThreshold, N, Edges, Source, Target, K) ->
    MidIndex = (Low + High) // 2,
    CurrentThreshold = lists:nth(MidIndex, UniqueWeights),

    case can_reach(N, Edges, Source, Target, K, CurrentThreshold) of
        true ->
            % Current threshold works, try for a smaller one
            NewMinThreshold = CurrentThreshold,
            BinarySearch(UniqueWeights, Low, MidIndex - 1, NewMinThreshold, N, Edges, Source, Target, K);
        false ->
            % Current threshold is too small, need a larger one
            BinarySearch(UniqueWeights, MidIndex + 1, High, MinThreshold, N, Edges, Source, Target, K)
    end.

% can_reach/6: Checks if target is reachable from source with at most K heavy edges given the threshold.
can_reach(N, Edges, Source, Target, K, Threshold) ->
    % State: {Node, HeavyCount}
